articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

article.headline: Wasteful Leipzig battle past Heidenheim to move into top four
article.description: RB Leipzig struck twice in the first half to edge past visitors Heidenheim 2-1 on Saturday and leapfrog Borussia Dortmund into fourth place in the Bundesliga.
article.linkText: Wasteful Leipzig battle past Heidenheim to move into top four

images.name: RB Leipzig vs. 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from RB Leipzig vs. 1. FC Heidenheim 1846, 12/02/2023

video.headline: RB Leipzig vs. 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 - Game Highlights
video.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from RB Leipzig vs. 1. FC Heidenheim 1846, 12/02/2023
video.title: RB Leipzig vs. 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 - Game Highlights
video.description: Watch the Game Highlights from RB Leipzig vs. 1. FC Heidenheim 1846, 12/02/2023

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC Highlight (RB Leipzig vs. 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 - Game Highlights) 2023/12/02 ESHEET SOTFULL

article.story: BERLIN, Dec 2 (Reuters) - RB Leipzig struck twice in the first half to edge past visitors Heidenheim 2-1 on Saturday and leapfrog Borussia Dortmund into fourth place in the Bundesliga. The hosts took a 29th-minute lead through a well-struck Lois Openda penalty and doubled it a minute before the break when David Raum charged down the left and crossed for Yusuf Poulsen to tap in. Heidenheim cut the deficit in first-half stoppage time, however, when Benedikt Gimber slid in at the far post to stab home from a corner with the Leipzig defence caught napping. In a dominant second half, the hosts should have scored more goals. They missed several golden chances, including two late one-on-ones for Openda against Heidenheim keeper Kevin Mueller and twice hit the woodwork. Leipzig moved to 26 points, two ahead of fifth-placed Dortmund, who take play leaders Bayer Leverkusen on Sunday. Second-placed Bayern Munich, on 32, will not be in action this weekend after heavy snowfall in the Bavarian capital forced the postponement of Sunday's match against Union Berlin. (Reporting by Karolos Grohmann, editing by Ed Osmond)
